claim_status: no-claim framework bake-off inventory only; no UI toolkit selection, no ADR-0014 approval, no equivalent adapter evidence, no accessibility readiness, no IME or keyboard proof, no page-surface approval, no trusted-chrome readiness, no release-path UI approval, no performance memory or energy claim, no license or provenance approval, and no production claim

external_observations.observation: Slint documentation shows ahead-of-time .slint compilation through slint_build, Winit backend access, platform abstraction, WGPU renderer integration examples, and texture import or rendering notifier hooks relevant to page-surface experiments.
external_observations.claim_boundary: This does not prove browser page-surface composition, accessibility coverage, licensing acceptance, or suitability for trusted chrome.

external_observations.observation: Slint offers GPLv3, royalty-free, and commercial licensing paths with attribution and embedded-system distinctions that require written Turing legal and distribution review.
external_observations.claim_boundary: This does not approve any Slint license path for Turing.

external_observations.observation: Vizia documentation shows a declarative Rust desktop GUI model with signals, events, CSS-style styling, Winit application setup, cross-platform window management, accessibility features, custom drawing, localization, and animation support.
external_observations.claim_boundary: This does not prove Vizia can handle Turing page surfaces, browser-scale accessibility, native IME behavior, or production stability.

external_observations.observation: Floem documentation shows fine-grained reactive signals, a persistent view tree, CSS-inspired styling, VirtualStack for large lists, GPU-backed renderers, and a CPU fallback path.
external_observations.claim_boundary: This does not prove Floem accessibility, IME, browser page-surface composition, or release-path maturity for Turing.

external_observations.source: https://gpui.rs/ and Zed GPUI documentation, retrieved 2026-07-18
external_observations.observation: GPUI documentation presents a Rust UI framework from the creators of Zed with examples and API documentation in Zed crates, making it a high-density comparison candidate.
external_observations.claim_boundary: This does not prove extraction cost, platform coverage, licensing, accessibility, or suitability for a standalone Turing browser shell.

external_observations.observation: Tauri architecture documentation describes desktop applications built with Rust tools and HTML rendered in a WebView.
external_observations.claim_boundary: This reinforces the release-shell exclusion of Tauri, WRY, system webviews, and runtime web UI for trusted chrome.

Spaces selector and address or command field

same benchmark hardware, package profile, and artifact rules

evidence_axes.name: Equivalent Adapter Scope
evidence_axes.required_output: Raw adapter run artifacts proving the same shell tasks, same UI model, same command contract, same fixtures, and same failure scenarios across Slint, Vizia, and Floem or GPUI.
evidence_axes.missing_status: No equivalent adapter implementation or raw run artifacts exist.

evidence_axes.name: Accessibility And Assistive Technology
evidence_axes.required_output: Platform accessibility tree snapshots, keyboard/focus traces, screen-reader transcripts, manual assistive-technology notes, contrast/motion results, and accessibility latency samples.
evidence_axes.missing_status: Only planning inventories exist; no reference-platform or manual assistive-technology evidence exists.

evidence_axes.name: IME, Keyboard, Text, Clipboard, And Localization
evidence_axes.required_output: IME composition, dead-key, international text, selection, clipboard, shortcut, focus, localization, zoom, and appearance fixture outputs.
evidence_axes.missing_status: No candidate has executable Turing fixture output for these workflows.

evidence_axes.name: Page Surface And Renderer Integration
evidence_axes.required_output: Typed page-surface handles, brokered surface handles, renderer frame source, resize/scale/damage/input/IME/accessibility/occlusion/capture/fault fixtures, and UI-GATE-7 evidence.
evidence_axes.missing_status: PB-005 has a checked planning inventory only.

evidence_axes.name: Crash, Renderer Hang, GPU Loss, And Recovery
evidence_axes.required_output: Fault-injection runs proving renderer hang, renderer crash, GPU device loss, UI crash, state restoration, no profile corruption, and no automatic replay of consequential actions.

evidence_axes.name: Performance, Memory, Binary, And Energy
evidence_axes.required_output: Cold/warm startup, input-to-present latency, frame-pacing, idle and 10/30/100-tab memory, allocation, binary/package contribution, wakeup, energy, and teardown measurements on the same reference hardware.

evidence_axes.name: License, Dependency, Unsafe, Native, And Provenance
evidence_axes.required_output: Dependency tree, license and notice review, advisory scan, unsafe/native/build-script/proc-macro review, source provenance, update cadence, maintainer health, and distribution obligation record.
evidence_axes.missing_status: Only qualitative candidate notes exist; no owner-accepted legal/provenance review exists.

evidence_axes.name: Replacement, Maintenance, And Exit Cost
evidence_axes.required_output: Adapter boundary proof, toolkit-state non-durability proof, migration estimate, version-upgrade rehearsal, rollback path, and owner coverage.
evidence_axes.missing_status: No candidate replacement rehearsal or upgrade/rollback evidence exists.

evidence_axes.name: Build, Iteration, Packaging, And Runtime Exclusions
evidence_axes.required_output: Clean build time, incremental build time, preview/hot-reload behavior, package contents, dynamic-library list, no webview/runtime JavaScript/Node/React/DOM/CSS parser proof, and reproducible artifact capture.
evidence_axes.missing_status: No package or clean-build candidate evidence exists.

disqualifiers.condition: Candidate requires Electron, Tauri, WRY, CEF, a system webview, Node, runtime React, runtime JavaScript, runtime DOM, or runtime CSS parser in trusted release chrome.
disqualifiers.reason: Trusted chrome cannot include a second browser engine, webview runtime, or script-controlled policy surface.

disqualifiers.condition: Candidate requires toolkit callbacks or toolkit state to decide navigation, permission, credential, profile, Plug-in, agent, persistence, update, or release authority.
disqualifiers.reason: The toolkit is presentation infrastructure; Turing services must revalidate typed commands and durable state.

disqualifiers.condition: Candidate cannot provide platform accessibility and manual assistive-technology evidence for core browser workflows.
disqualifiers.reason: Browser chrome must be keyboard and assistive-technology operable before release-path UI approval.

disqualifiers.condition: Candidate has no reviewed path for page-surface composition, input routing, IME, accessibility composition, capture, occlusion, crash, and GPU-loss behavior.
disqualifiers.reason: Ordinary widget success does not prove browser shell suitability.

disqualifiers.condition: Candidate license, provenance, native dependency, build script, binary package, or update cadence cannot be accepted under Turing distribution and security policy.
disqualifiers.reason: A framework cannot enter release code without owner-accepted dependency and legal review.

unsupported_boundaries: no performance memory or energy claim
